definition: adverse means negative, bad, or harmful
example: he had an adverse reaction to the drug
this may adversely affect her performance in school

definition: an allergy is a medical condition in which you become ill or your skin becomes covered with red marks when you eat, breathe, or touch a particular thing
example: a dust allergy

definition: an allergy is a medical condition in which you become ill or your skin becomes covered with red marks when you eat, breathe, or touch a particular thing. If you are allergic to something, you are affected by an allergy
example: I can’t eat prawns, I’m allergic

definition: antibiotics are drugs that are given to people to cure infections and to kill bacteria
example: the infection should clear up with antibiotics
an antibiotic drug

definition: a bruise is a dark mark you get on your body if you are hit
example: she had a nasty bruise on her face
I fell and bruised my leg
I fell and bruised my leg

definition: if an illness clears up, or if something clears it up, it becomes better
example: the rash should clear up in a day or two

definition: consciousness is the condition of being awake and being aware of what is happening around you. The adjective is conscious
example: did you actually lose consciousness?
he was fully conscious during the operation
she was knocked unconscious when she fell

definition: if you have diarrhoea, a lot of liquid waste comes out of your bottom because you are ill
example: have you had any diarrhoea at all?

definition: if you feel dizzy, you feel as if things around you are spinning and you are going to fall over
example: I feel dizzy – my head is spinning
have you had any dizziness at all?

definition: dust is the very small pieces of dry dirt that cover surfaces
example: the dust is making me sneeze
the road was hot and dusty
you need to dust those shelves

definition: a fluid is a liquid
example: bodily fluid

definition: if an injury heals, or if something heals it, it recovers and becomes well again
example: it’s quite a nasty cut. It’ll take a while to heal

definition: if you have an infection, there is a bacteria or virus in part of your body that is making you ill. You can also say that a part of your body is infected.
example: your throat is very sore, but it doesn’t look infected
I had a nasty chest infection
it’s important not to infect other people

definition: if an illness or disease is infectious, it can move from one person to another
example: it’s a highly infectious illness

definition: medication is a drug that you take to cure an illness
example: are you on any medication?
patients in hospice care are heavily medicated
patients in hospice care are heavily medicated

definition: if a person or a part of the body mends, or if someone or something mends them, they become healthy again after an injury or illness
example: she has a small break in her wrist but it will soon mend
if you take the doctor’s advice you’ll soon be on the mend

definition: if an illness or injury is nasty, it is very bad and unpleasant
example: you’ve got a nasty cough

definition: a reaction to something is a particular effect that happens as a result of it
example: she had an allergic reaction to the prawns
some patients react badly to the medication

definition: if a part of your body is sore, it is painful and uncomfortable
example: I’ve got a blocked nose and a horrible sore throat
the soreness should only last for a few days

definition: if you sprain a joint in your body, you hurt it by turning it too much
example: I think you’ve sprained your ankle
it’s probably just sprained, but we’ll do an X-ray to check
I’ve hurt my ankle but I think it’s only a sprain

definition: if you stick out a part of your body, you push it out and away from the rest of your body
example: stick out your hand, I want to give you something

definition: a stitch is a short piece of thread sewn in a piece of cloth. In medicine, stitches are short pieces of thread used for joining someone’s skin together after it has been cut or injured
example: I think you’re going to need stitches
the surgeon managed to stitch Joel’s finger back together
the surgeon managed to stitch Joel’s finger back together

definition: if you swallow something, it moves from your mouth into your stomach
example: you shouldn’t swallow chewing gum
she gobbled up the whole cake in just a couple of swallows

definition: if a part of your body is swollen, it is bigger than usual because of an injury or illness
example: his knee was painful and swollen
my arm had swollen up from the bee sting
put some ice on it to reduce the swelling

definition: symptoms are signs in your body that show you have an illness
example: apart from a headache, do you have any other symptoms?

definition: your throat is the area at the back of your mouth and inside your neck
example: initial symptoms include fever and a mild sore throat

definition: if you throw up or throw something up, food or drink comes up from your stomach and out of your mouth. Throw up is informal, and a more polite word is vomit
example: I think I’m going to throw up

definition: a virus is a very small living thing that can enter your body and cause a disease or illness
example: he picked up a nasty  virus
flu is a highly contagious viral infection

definition: weight is a measurement of how heavy a person or thing is
example: he’s been trying to lose weight
I’m not sure how much I weigh at the moment

definition: if something is addictive, you cannot stop taking it or doing it once you start. You become addicted to it. A person who is addicted is an addict
example: coffee is very addictive
I’m completely addicted to those new chocolate bars
he became a drug addict
an addiction to sleeping pills

definition: to adjust something means to change it slightly in order to make it better or more effective
example: you’ll probably need to adjust the seatbelt
these seats are adjustable
no cost of living adjustment is included

definition: if you balance two contrasting things or parts of something, you give each one an equal level of importance or strength
example: we have to balance Jill’s needs against the needs of the rest of the group

definition: imbalance between something
example: it can be hard to find the right balance between work and leisure

definition: imbalance between something
example: an imbalance of political power

definition: a basis for something is an important fact or idea that supports it and that it can develop from
example: these stories have no basis in fact

definition: to block something means to put an object across or in it which stops something else from moving through or along it
example: eating too much fat can block your arteries
he’s trying to fix a blockage in the drains
there must be a block somewhere in the pipeline

definition: a bug is an illness caused by bacteria and which is not usually serious. Bug is an informal word
example: a sickness/stomach bug

definition: if you crack the joints in your hands, you pull or bend your fingers so that they make a loud sound
example: don’t crack your knuckles – it’s horrible!

definition: to cure someone, or cure their illness, means to stop them from being affected by an illness. An illness that can be cured is curable
example: if the disease is caught early it is often curable

definition: to cure someone, or cure their illness, means to stop them from being affected by an illness. A disease that cannot be cured is incurable
example: antibiotics can’t cure a common cold
antibiotics can’t cure a common cold
he’s got an incurable medical condition

definition: if you do something gently, you do it carefully and without a lot of force
example: wash your face gently twice a day
apply the cream in gentle circular movements

definition: incidentally is a word used for adding information which is useful but not directly related to what has just been said, or for suddenly introducing a new subject
example: research has failed to find any connection between eating fatty foods and acne. Incidentally, washing too much can make the condition worse

definition: if a person is irritable, they become angry or annoyed very easily
example: the longer he had to wait, the more irritable he became
she also developed unpleasant symptoms of agitation and irritability
‘there’s no sugar in this coffee,’ she said irritably

definition: an issue is an important subject affecting society that people discuss
example: abortion has always been a rather controversial issue

definition: your joints are the parts of your body where two bones meet and can move together, for example your knee
example: a swollen elbow joint

definition: if someone or something links two things, or if the two things are linked, there is a connection between them, for example because one thing causes the other
example: scientists now link such illnesses to the amount of sugary or fatty food we consume
there’s no link between intelligence and social status

definition: if something modernises, or if you modernise it, it changes because methods, equipment, ideas, etc are replaced by new ones
example: they’ve got plans to modernise the old factory
a modernisation plan was started in early 1940
he is considered a moderniser who is interested in embracing new cultures and ideas

definition: a myth is an idea or fact that many people believe but that is actually not true
example: contrary to popular myth, women do not have a longer life expectancy than men
have you ever seen this mythical girlfriend of his?

definition: if you question something, you say that you have doubts about it and think it might be wrong
example: some doctors have even questioned if there is any link between cholesterol levels and heart disease

definition: regular describes something that happens or is done often
example: if you want to stay healthy you should take regular exercise
if you want to stay healthy you should eat well and exercise regularly
she was travelling by train with increasing regularity

definition: to rub something means to press and move your hands or an object over its surface. If you rub a part of your body, you press and move your hands over it
example: wash your face without soap and don’t rub the skin too hard
sit down and I’ll give your back a rub

definition: if something speeds up, or if you speed it up, it moves or happens more quickly
example: what can I do to speed up my computer?

definition: if you suffer pain, you feel it in your body or mind. If you suffer from an illness, you are badly affected by it
example: you shouldn’t suffer too much discomfort
the high pollen count is very bad news for allergy sufferers

definition: vitamins are substances which are necessary to keep your body healthy and can be found naturally in food or eaten in the form of pills. Each vitamin is given a name using a letter of the alphabet
example: vitamin C tablets

definition: if you wrap up, you wear enough clothes to keep you warm
example: wrap up warm – it’s freezing outside!

definition: a bang is a sudden, short, loud noise
example: the horse got scared by a loud bang from across the fields
minutes later we heard the front gate bang shut

definition: if you faint, you suddenly become unconscious for a short period of time
example: I felt dizzy and just fainted
I suddenly felt faint and had to sit down

definition: a frame is an object made of something rigid that goes around the edge of a picture, door, window etc and keeps the picture, door, or window in place
example: the house had been built with a modern timber frame

definition: if you go off the road, you lose control of a vehicle and cannot stay on the road
example: the road was icy and there was a risk we’d go off the road

definition: poison is a substance that can make you ill or kill you if you eat, drink, or breathe it. Poisoning is when a person is affected by poison
example: I ate a hot dog from a stand and I think it gave me food poisoning
rat poison
rat poison
some types of mushroom are poisonous

definition: a steep hill, slope, etc rises very quickly and so is difficult to go up or down
example: be careful how you walk, the steps are pretty steep just here
the path climbs steeply, but walkers are rewarded with spectacular views

definition: if an insect stings you, it sticks a sharp part of its body into you and hurts you
example: this bee stung me and my whole arm swelled up
it gave me a painful sting

definition: a test is an examination of someone’s body to check that they are healthy or find out if they have an illness
example: a test for diabetes
she’s being tested for diabetes
